title: Occupancy Classification
text: Structures or portions of structures shall be classified with respect to occupancy in one or more of the groups listed in Sections 303 through 312: Assembly (A-1 through A-5), Business (B), Educational (E), Factory (F-1, F-2), High Hazard (H-1 through H-5), Institutional (I-1 through I-4), Mercantile (M), Residential (R-1 through R-4), Storage (S-1, S-2), and Utility (U). A room or space classified as part of more than one occupancy shall comply with the requirements applicable to each occupancy.

title: Allowable Building Height and Area
text: Allowable building height in feet and stories is determined by Table 504.3 and Table 504.4 based on occupancy group and construction type (Types I-A, I-B, II-A, II-B, III-A, III-B, IV-HT, V-A, V-B). Allowable building area per Section 506 is based on Table 506.2 with increases permitted for frontage and sprinklers per 506.3.

title: Construction Types and Fire-Resistance Ratings
text: Buildings shall be classified as Type I, II, III, IV, or V construction. Types I and II are noncombustible. Type III has exterior walls of noncombustible material; interior may be of any material. Type IV (Heavy Timber) requires exterior walls of noncombustible and large solid or laminated timber interior members. Type V permits any code-permitted material. Fire-resistance ratings for primary structural frame, exterior walls, and interior bearing walls are per Table 601.

title: Fire-Resistance Rating Based on Fire Separation Distance
text: Fire-resistance rating of exterior walls based on fire separation distance to lot lines per Table 705.5: 0 to less than 5 ft requires 1- or 2-hour rating depending on construction type and occupancy. Greater than 30 ft generally requires 0 hours. Walls less than 5 ft from lot line may also be limited in unprotected openings (Table 705.8).

title: Occupant Load
text: The occupant load of a space shall be the actual number of occupants, but not less than that determined by dividing the floor area by the occupant load factor in Table 1004.5. Common factors: assembly fixed seats = number of seats; assembly without fixed seats concentrated = 7 net sf/occupant; business = 150 gross sf/occupant; educational classroom = 20 net sf/occupant; mercantile = 60 gross sf/occupant; residential = 200 gross sf/occupant; storage = 300 gross sf/occupant.

title: Number of Exits Per Story
text: Each story and occupied roof shall have the number of separate and distinct exits required by Table 1006.3.1 based on occupant load and occupancy. Two exits required for most occupancies when occupant load exceeds 49 (or 29 for H occupancies). Three exits required when occupant load exceeds 500; four when over 1000.

title: Egress Door Size
text: The minimum width of each door opening shall be sufficient for the occupant load thereof and shall provide a clear width of 32 inches (813 mm). Clear openings of doorways with swinging doors shall be measured between the face of the door and the stop, with the door open 90 degrees. The maximum width of a swinging door leaf shall be 48 inches (1219 mm) nominal.

title: Stairway Width and Capacity
text: The width of stairways shall be determined as specified in Section 1005.1 but not less than 44 inches (1118 mm). Exceptions include: stairways serving an occupant load of less than 50 may be 36 inches (914 mm) wide; spiral stairways per 1011.10; and aisle stairs per 1029.13.2.

title: Stair Riser Height and Tread Depth
text: Stair riser heights shall be 7 inches (178 mm) maximum and 4 inches (102 mm) minimum. Stair tread depths shall be 11 inches (279 mm) minimum. The riser height shall be measured vertically between the leading edges of adjacent treads. Riser height and tread depth within any flight of stairs shall not vary more than 3/8 inch (9.5 mm) between the smallest and largest.

title: Exit Access Travel Distance
text: Exit access travel distance shall not exceed the values in Table 1017.2: with sprinklers, 250 ft for Group A, B, E, F-1, M, R, S-1, and U; 400 ft for F-2 and S-2; 200 ft for H-1 through H-3. Without sprinklers, values reduce significantly (e.g., 200 ft for B, 75 ft for H-1).

title: Corridor Width
text: The minimum width of corridors specified in Table 1020.2 shall be as determined in Section 1005.1, but the minimum width shall be 44 inches (1118 mm). Exceptions: 24 inches for access to electrical equipment; 36 inches for occupant load less than 50; 36 inches in within a dwelling unit; 72 inches in Group E with corridor occupant load of 100 or more; 96 inches in Group I-2.

title: Minimum Ceiling Height
text: Occupiable spaces, habitable spaces and corridors shall have a ceiling height of not less than 7 feet 6 inches (2286 mm). Bathrooms, toilet rooms, kitchens, storage rooms, and laundry rooms shall have a ceiling height of not less than 7 feet (2134 mm).

title: Required Live Loads
text: Buildings and other structures shall be designed and constructed to support the uniformly distributed live loads in Table 1607.1: residential one- and two-family = 40 psf habitable, 30 psf attic with limited storage; offices = 50 psf; light manufacturing = 125 psf; assembly fixed seats = 60 psf; assembly without fixed seats = 100 psf; stairs and exit corridors = 100 psf; mechanical/electrical equipment rooms = 125 psf.

title: Wind Loads
text: Buildings and structures, including the main wind force-resisting system and components and cladding, shall be designed and constructed to resist wind loads in accordance with ASCE 7 Chapters 26 through 30. Risk Category I, II, III, or IV per Table 1604.5. Basic wind speeds (Vult) from Figure 1609.3.

title: Seismic Design Requirements
text: Every structure, and portion thereof, including nonstructural components, shall be designed and constructed to resist the effects of earthquake motions in accordance with ASCE 7, excluding Chapter 14 and Appendix 11A. Seismic Design Category (SDC) A through F determined by Section 1613 and ASCE 7 Section 11.6 based on site soil class and mapped spectral response.

title: Site Grading
text: The ground immediately adjacent to the foundation shall be sloped away from the building at a slope of not less than 1 unit vertical in 20 units horizontal (5-percent slope) for a minimum distance of 10 feet (3048 mm) measured perpendicular to the face of the wall.
